Alternate Picking Mastery: 3 Notes Per String.

The Problem And The Fix

Master Alternate Picking: Build Speed The Right Way.

Tired of trying to play faster and just getting sloppier? Most players hit a wall because they're training speed on top of bad habits. This is the exact process I use with students to rebuild technique from the ground up.

01
You're Practicing Hard. But It's Not Clicking.
No matter how many reps you put in, alternate picking still feels off. You tense up. You miss notes. You get fast but messy. And the more you push, the worse it gets.
02
The Real Problem Isn't Speed.
Playing faster without fixing fundamentals only locks in mistakes. You might nail a lick one day, then fall apart the next. That inconsistency isn't your talent. It's your training method.
03
Train for Precision. Speed Will Follow.
Alternate Picking Mastery is a step-by-step system designed to rebuild hand synchronisation from the ground up. You'll train with control, not chaos, and develop true, reliable speed.
04
Results You Can Feel (and Hear).
Faster runs. Cleaner notes. Confidence when you pick up the guitar. You'll finally feel in control and stop second-guessing every note.
05
Ready to Practice Smarter, Not Harder?
If you're done spinning your wheels, it's time to train with a method built to work. Join Alternate Picking Mastery and unlock the speed and precision you've been chasing.

FAQ

Got Questions? We've Got Answers.

How much time do I need to practice per day?
The full routine takes around 90 minutes per day. If you can't commit that much time, you can shorten certain sections by alternating the starting pick strokes. For best results, work toward the full routine.
When will I see results?
You'll notice a difference within a few days. Everything flows and sounds better. After a few weeks you'll start playing faster and faster without mistakes. After that, the sky is the limit depending on how much time you invest.
What if I get stuck and need help?
Post a message under any lesson and I'll help you out as soon as I can. You're not on your own with this.
Is this exactly what you did to build your technique?
No, this is a more streamlined version of what I did. You don't have to go through the years of trial and error I went through to find what works. I wish I'd had something like this when I was starting out.

Stop Practicing Hard. Start Practicing Smart.

Instant access. Self-paced. 60-day picking progress guarantee.
